title: pi-astack settings
description: Configuration schema for pi-astack extensions. Lives at ~/.pi/agent/pi-astack-settings.json. Each top-level key namespaces one extension; missing sections fall back to that extension's hardcoded DEFAULTS. To enable IDE autocomplete + validation, add "$schema": "./path/to/pi-astack-settings.schema.json" as the first field in your settings file.

$schema.description: Path or URL to this schema file. Used by IDEs for validation and autocomplete; ignored at runtime.

_comment.description: Free-form comment field. JSON has no native comments; use this (or any underscore-prefixed key) for inline documentation.

llmAudit.description: LLM audit settings (extensions/llm-audit/ and extensions/_shared/llm-audit.ts). The backgroundBudget shell guards shared auditStreamSimple calls before the model is invoked. It uses cheap process-local counters keyed by turn/module/operation/model when a causal anchor exists, and by bounded process-window/module/operation/model when no turn identity is available.

rotation.description: Process-local serialized JSONL append with size/age generation rotation. Rotation alone uses the shared O_EXCL lock; lock or rotation failures fail open to the active audit.jsonl.

backgroundBudget.description: Deterministic budget shell for background pi-ai streamSimple calls routed through auditStreamSimple. Limits are checked before the LLM call and fail closed with BackgroundLlmBudgetExceededError. Token counts are estimated from prompt characters and are intended as cost-control guards, not provider billing truth.

enabled.description: Master switch. false disables all budget checks while keeping normal llm-audit logging.

maxPromptChars.description: Maximum prompt characters for a single auditStreamSimple call. 0 disables this single-call character cap.

maxPromptEstimatedTokens.description: Maximum estimated prompt tokens for a single auditStreamSimple call. Estimated as ceil(promptChars / 4). 0 disables this single-call token cap.

perOperationMaxCallsPerTurn.description: Maximum auditStreamSimple calls per turn/module/operation/model key. 0 disables this call-count cap. Without a causal turn identity, the same cap is process-local within a bounded fallback window.

perOperationMaxEstimatedTokensPerTurn.description: Maximum cumulative estimated prompt tokens per turn/module/operation/model key. Estimated as ceil(promptChars / 4). 0 disables this cumulative token cap. Without a causal turn identity, the same cap is process-local within a bounded fallback window.

canonicalGitRuntime.description: Fail-closed local Git convergence runtime. It validates whole L1, recovers active local drain v2 episodes, publishes exact temporary-index commits by CAS, and converges the shared index. Device remote delivery is outside this runtime.

enabled.description: Explicit activation switch. Production remains disabled until separately authorized.

mode.description: Pinned local-only protocol mode. Unknown modes and residual transport fields fail closed.

abrain.description: Abrain / vault settings (extensions/abrain/). Handles strict project binding, vault release, and Windows vault-backed bash injection guard.

windowsVaultBashPath.description: Windows-only override for vault-backed bash injection. Default is Git for Windows bash.exe. Set this only when using another supported Git Bash/MSYS2 path; WSL and Cygwin remain unsupported for Windows-hosted pi.

modelCurator.description: Whitelist + capability hints for chat models. The curator filters pi-ai's hundreds of built-in model definitions to a curated keep-list per provider, then injects a markdown capability table into the main session system prompt on every turn.

providers.description: Keep-list per provider. Keys are provider names; values are arrays of provider-native model IDs to retain. All other models in that provider are removed from the registry. Providers not listed here are left untouched. Concrete model IDs belong in pi-astack-settings.json, not in this schema.

additionalProperties.description: Provider-native model IDs to keep for this provider. Choose models by the capability needed for the configured tier or call point.

hints.description: One-line capability hints shown next to each chat model in the system prompt table. Keys use 'provider/modelId'. Style: present tense, max ~15 words, and describe concrete capabilities rather than recommending a specific model globally.

examples.provider-a/strong-reasoning-model: Long-context reasoning, architecture critique, and security review.
examples.provider-a/fast-classification-model: Low-latency classification and short structured summaries.
examples.provider-b/independent-review-model: Independent cross-vendor review and adversarial checking.

imageGen.description: Image generation models. NOT registered as pi Models (they cannot do chat) — listed in a separate 'Image generation' section of the system prompt as informational hints only. Keys are image model IDs from the configured provider.

examples.image-model-id: Supports text-to-image generation and image-to-image editing through imagine().

tiers.description: REQUIRED. Authoritative model-tier roster. Drives the 'Tier roster' section the model-curator injects into the system prompt and any T0 dispatch planning. Keys are tier names (commonly 'flagship' / 'flagship_candidate' / 'standard' / 'fast'); values are objects with `label`, optional `description`, and `models` (array of `provider/modelId`). Tiers are NOT runtime fallback chains; they are a capability ranking for LLM selection guidance. Business call points (memory, sediment, vision, workflow, goal, compaction, imagine) continue to read their own per-role model fields, NOT this roster. Selection rule: when adding flagship members, prefer cross-vendor + cross-architecture; do not count two same-vendor models as two independent voters. A `flagship_candidate` tier, when present, means specialist/under-validation voices: do not count them as primary T0 voters in 3-way blind audits.

flagship.label: T0 — cost-blind flagship reasoning
flagship.description: Critical agentic dispatch surface. Cost is never a consideration.

openaiServiceTier.description: OpenAI service_tier injection (extensions/openai-service-tier/). Adds the official OpenAI request body field `service_tier` for explicitly allowlisted OpenAI-compatible models via before_provider_request. Intended for provider scheduling experiments without patching pi core. Env PI_ASTACK_DISABLE_OPENAI_SERVICE_TIER=1 wins over this setting.

enabled.description: Master switch. When false, the extension leaves provider payloads unchanged. Env PI_ASTACK_DISABLE_OPENAI_SERVICE_TIER=1 always disables injection.

disableForSubAgent.description: Skip service_tier injection for dispatch-spawned sub-agent sessions. Default true keeps critical reviews and background workers on normal provider scheduling unless explicitly changed later.

serviceTier.description: OpenAI service_tier value to inject. `fast` is accepted as a local alias and normalized to official OpenAI `priority`; all emitted requests use official values.

modelAllowlist.description: Exact `provider/modelId` allowlist for models that support this provider-specific scheduling option. Empty list means inactive even when enabled, so a config typo fails closed instead of enabling service_tier broadly.

modelFallback.description: Multi-model fallback chain with retry (extensions/model-fallback/). Asymmetric policy: the **initial** model gets pi's full retry budget before fallback kicks in (retry count auto-read from pi settings.json#retry.maxRetries, no separate config needed); **each fallback model** gets exactly 1 attempt — any error switches to the next configured model immediately. When all fallback models are tried and all fail, the extension stops. For an aggressive ten-attempt retry profile with exponential delays, set pi settings.json#retry.maxRetries=9 + retry.baseDelayMs=1000; the extension auto-aligns.

fallbackModels.description: Ordered list of `provider/modelId` strings. When the initial model exhausts its retries, the extension walks this list in order, picking the first un-tried entry that exists in pi's modelRegistry and has configured auth. Entries missing from the registry or without auth are logged as warnings and skipped. The currently-active model (whatever it is when the error happens) is auto-added to the tried set, so it's safe to include it in the list. Empty list = retry behavior only, no fallback.

sediment.description: Sediment writer settings (extensions/sediment/). Provides explicit MEMORY writes plus LLM auto-write for project-bound memory and abrain world targets, including event-first Knowledge/Constraint writes and derived projections. ADR 0016 curator model for semantic decisions; sensitive-info sanitizer + storage integrity gates remain hard. Extractor and Curator have independent model configs (2026-05-11 audit).

enabled.description: Enable agent_end sediment hook (explicit MEMORY: block extractor + LLM auto-write curator lane). Both extraction paths are fully implemented (Phase 1.4 + ADR 0016). Default false — must be explicitly opted in.

gitCommit.description: Whether writer attempts best-effort git add + git commit after markdown atomic write. Git failure does not roll back markdown.

defaultConfidence.description: Default confidence for writer smoke/deterministic provisional entries.

minWindowChars.description: Minimum rendered run-window size. Smaller windows are skipped by future extractor logic.

maxWindowChars.description: Maximum rendered run-window size. Raised from 200K to 350K (2026-05-11) with per-entry truncation at maxEntryChars. 350K chars is about 87.5K tokens, so choose extractor models with enough context headroom.

maxWindowEntries.description: Maximum number of newest session entries considered for a sediment run window.

maxEntryChars.description: Per-entry character cap for toolResult and bashExecution entries. Head 85% + tail 15% preserved; middle truncated. Prevents a single large tool output (e.g. 150K file read) from dominating the extractor window. User/assistant/compaction entries pass through untouched.

extractorModel.description: Model used by the LLM extractor (transcript → MEMORY blocks). Needs strong long-context reading comprehension, bilingual text handling, structured extraction, and adversarial-prompt resistance. Empty default means fail-closed unless configured in pi-astack-settings.json.

extractorTimeoutMs.description: Timeout for LLM extractor model calls. Default 20 minutes because sediment runs fire-and-forget in the background; provider blips should surface as footer/audit state, not foreground blocking.

extractorMaxRetries.description: Max retries for LLM extractor calls. Default 0 to avoid repeated extraction costs on large windows.

classifierModel.description: Model used by the active-correction classifier (conversation window → CorrectionSignal). Needs low-latency reading comprehension and reliable structured output; deep reasoning is not required. Empty default means fail-closed unless configured in pi-astack-settings.json.

ruleContradictionConfirmModel.description: Optional provider/model ref for LLM confirmation before rule CONTRADICT candidates can demote a rule to contested. Empty means runtime falls back to sediment.classifierModel. Concrete model IDs belong in pi-astack-settings.json.

classifierTimeoutMs.description: Timeout for active-correction classifier model calls. Default matches extractorTimeoutMs so background provider latency does not create noisy false-fail audit rows.

extractorMaxCandidates.description: Runaway-detection cap: outputs with more candidates than this are marked too_many_candidates and skipped (LLM likely ignoring the prompt). The extractor prompt asks the LLM for at most two MEMORY blocks per response; a healthy run produces 1–2, a slightly noisy run produces 3–4. Default 5 catches true runaway without flagging benign overshoot.

extractorAuditRawChars.description: Number of raw LLM output characters stored in manual extractor previews. The live auto-write audit uses autoWriteRawAuditChars.

curatorModel.description: Model used by the curator (candidate + neighbors → create/update/merge/skip decision). Needs small-context entity resolution, conservative lifecycle judgment, and reliable structured output. Empty default means fail-closed unless configured in pi-astack-settings.json. Split from extractorModel (2026-05-11 audit).

curatorTimeoutMs.description: Timeout for curator model calls. Default 20 minutes for parity with background extractor/classifier calls.

curatorMaxRetries.description: Max retries for curator calls. Default 1 to recover from occasional JSON parse errors (highly structured output).

constraintShadowCompiler.description: ADR 0039 P1/P4 constraint shadow compiler report and runtime auto-refresh. Manual entries may call a real LLM and write only ~/.abrain/.state/sediment/constraint-shadow/** artifacts; auto-refresh is guarded separately.

enabled.description: Explicit guard for the constraint shadow compiler. Auto-refresh also requires autoRefresh.enabled.

model.description: Optional provider/model ref for the constraint compiler. Empty means the manual entry falls back to sediment.curatorModel.

l2OutputRoot.description: ADR0039 Constraint L2 (4×T0 NS-2/FIX-1). When 'repo', each successful compile 固化s the validated decision as an immutable L1 constraint-projection event and renders the deterministic L2 view to git-tracked l2/views/constraint/latest/compiled-view.md (SHADOW: runtime injection still reads .state; no read-flip). 'state' (default) = current behavior, no 固化, no l2 write.

timeoutMs.description: Timeout for the constraint compiler model call.

maxRetries.description: Transport retry count for the constraint compiler model call. Does not retry to repair malformed JSON.

maxCompileRetries.description: Validation/parse-feedback retry count for the constraint compiler. These retries re-prompt with the exact compile/validate error and are distinct from transport retries.

escalationModelRef.description: Optional provider/model ref used only for the final compile retry attempt. Empty means keep the primary constraint compiler model for every attempt.

maxPromptChars.description: Optional prompt character budget. 0 means no script-level cap; positive values fail closed before calling the model.

mergedSourceVerifier.description: Default-off ADR0039 merged_source verifier generator. Produces a data-only shadow sidecar for audit/review; it does not affect validationHash and is never read by runtime injection.

enabled.description: Explicit opt-in for generating merged-source verifier sidecars. false preserves existing compiler and auto-refresh behavior.

model.description: Optional provider/model ref for the verifier. Empty falls back to the constraint compiler model.

maxPromptChars.description: Optional verifier prompt character budget. 0 falls back to autoRefresh.maxPromptChars for auto-refresh, then constraintShadowCompiler.maxPromptChars when available, then no cap.

autoRefresh.description: ADR 0039 P4 runtime refresh after successful Constraint Evidence Event append. It is debounce/single-flight guarded and writes only derived constraint-shadow artifacts.

enabled.description: Explicit runtime guard for live shadow refresh. false keeps manual-only operation.

debounceMs.description: Delay before a scheduled auto-refresh run starts.

minIntervalMs.description: Minimum interval between completed auto-refresh runs.

eventStaleAfterMs.description: Derived shadow age threshold used by runtime/reconcile freshness checks.

maxPromptChars.description: Optional prompt budget for auto-refresh. 0 means use constraintShadowCompiler.maxPromptChars.

constraintEvidenceEventWriter.description: ADR 0039 runtime append of Constraint Evidence Events from the existing sediment agent_end pipeline. event_first may reject legacy mutation when append fails and fallback is disabled.

enabled.description: Explicit runtime guard. Keep this key present in pi-astack-settings.json as a rollback switch.

mode.description: parallel_legacy appends events alongside the legacy path; event_first treats successful append as the precondition for legacy mutation unless fallback is enabled.

legacyFallbackOnEventFailure.description: When true, append failure may fall back to the legacy writer. Set false for fail-closed event_first operation.

legacyRuleWriteOnSuccessfulEvent.description: When true, successful Constraint Evidence append still runs the legacy Tier-1 rules writer. Set false to isolate old write-time adjudication while keeping this rollback switch explicit.

tier2RulesLegacyWriteGate.description: Gate for Tier-2 curator rules-zone legacy rule writes. observe audits caller/scope/slug without changing behavior; block returns terminal skipped for Tier-2 rules-zone calls only; off disables this gate.

mode.description: off disables the gate; observe records audit rows and allows writes; block returns skipped before legacy rules mutation for callers that pass Tier-2 rules-zone context.

knowledgeEvidenceEventWriter.description: ADR 0039 runtime append of Knowledge Evidence Events from sediment writer operations. It supports parallel legacy mode and fail-closed event_first mode.

enabled.description: Explicit runtime guard for Knowledge Evidence Event append.

mode.description: parallel_legacy writes legacy markdown first; event_first appends evidence before canonical mutation and compensates projection after later failures.

legacyFallbackOnEventFailure.description: When true, append failure may fall back to the legacy writer. Set false for fail-closed event_first operation.

legacyMarkdownWriteOnSuccessfulEvent.description: When true, event_first still writes legacy markdown after a successful event append. Set false only for projection-only observation: successful event writes skip legacy markdown mutation while preserving positive writer statuses.

knowledgeProjector.description: ADR 0039 Knowledge Evidence Event projector and read overlay. It writes only derived files under ~/.abrain/.state/sediment/knowledge-projection/**.

enabled.description: Explicit guard for Knowledge projection.

hotOverlayEnabled.description: When true, memory read tools append projection stores after canonical stores.

projectOnWrite.description: When true, successful Knowledge Evidence Event append refreshes the derived projection immediately.

maxReadBytes.description: Maximum projection bytes read per file by read-side consumers that enforce a bound.

l2OutputRoot.description: ADR 0039 B1. Where the Knowledge L2 projection is written. 'state' keeps the gitignored runtime-cache location ~/.abrain/.state/sediment/knowledge-projection (current default). 'repo' moves it to the git-trackable namespace ~/.abrain/l2/views/knowledge; B1 keeps it shadow until B2 proves deterministic projection and B3 removes the gitignore. Explicit rollback flag.

projectionMode.description: ADR 0039 B2. How a Knowledge L2 entry is projected. 'single' (default) lets one triggering event overwrite one markdown file. 'topo' aggregates all events for a (scope, slug) identity, topologically sorts the causal-parent DAG, and deterministically folds them to one entry; a single event degenerates byte-identically to 'single'. Explicit rollback flag.

canonicalReadMode.description: ADR 0039 Phase C truth-face inversion. 'legacy' (default/rollback) = legacy markdown wins; projection is appended last as a bounded overlay and loses dedup. 'projection_with_legacy_fallback' = the UNBOUNDED stable-view projection (l2/views/knowledge) is inserted at the front and wins via first-store-wins, with legacy kept in the pool to fill slugs the projection lacks. 'projection_only' = stable view only; legacy leaves the winning pool (doctor-visible). The read flag is hot (re-read each loadEntries). Any value other than 'legacy' is gated by recorded multi-T0 consensus plus a preflight (pi restart onto repo root, reproject, blocker quality gates, reconcile/prepush PASS). Explicit rollback flag.

hotOverlay.description: ADR 0039 B-prep blocker3 (section 6 bounded hot overlay). Defensive caps on the projection OVERLAY reader. The freshest entries within the shared count+token budget are kept; overflow is recorded to .state/sediment/knowledge-projection/overlay-budget.jsonl. Bounds the overlay role only; the post-flip stable view is a separate unbounded primary store.

maxEntries.description: Max overlay entries (count cap). Freshest-by-mtime kept on overflow.

maxTokens.description: Max overlay tokens, estimated as stat-size/4 (no file read).

deadlineMs.description: Wall-clock budget for the overlay enumeration in milliseconds.

aggregatorModel.description: Model used by the aggregator v1 skeptical-historian LLM pass (ADR 0025 §4.3 Phase C, 2026-05-28; v1.1 L1 evolution self-state 2026-05-31; v1.2 identity convergence 2026-06-03). Reasoning-heavy task over 9 input feeds including evolution_hypotheses; choose a model with strong long-context synthesis, skepticism, and structured output. Empty/invalid falls back to curatorModel. Aggregator runs are debounced to about 24h.

aggregatorTimeoutMs.description: Timeout for aggregator LLM calls. Default 10 minutes — generous because aggregator is fire-and-forget background work.

aggregatorMaxRetries.description: Transport-level retries for aggregator LLM (HTTP network blips). MUST NOT be used as prompt-level retry to repair malformed JSON — forbidden by ADR 0024 §3 + v1 prompt §5 C6 (parse failure → degraded fallback, never re-ask LLM).

autoLlmWriteEnabled.description: Tristate switch for ADR 0024/0025 sediment auto-write. true = full pipeline (classifier + extractor + curator + writer). "staging-only"/"staging" = classifier still observes and may write provisional staging, but durable auto-write is skipped. false = hard kill switch; classifier also stops. Runtime also accepts string "true"/"false" for backward-compatible config parsing.

autoWriteRawAuditChars.description: Maximum raw LLM extractor output stored in each auto_write audit row. Unlike `extractorAuditRawChars` (preview-only, default 1000) this one is meant to be large enough for full forensic replay; only truncate when raw output exceeds the cap.

skipContinuationSanitize.description: When true, skip credential sanitization in the continuation-call path. Default false keeps sanitizer ON; only use true for air-gapped deployments where extractor provider cannot exfiltrate leaked keys.

rulesAsReadonlyNeighborsEnabled.description: ADR 0028 PR1 flag. When true, existing abrain rules are appended to curator neighbor context as read-only references for dedup/lifecycle awareness. Existing rules cannot be updated, archived, merged, superseded, or deleted through the sediment auto-write path. Default true provides the rule-neighbor context required for ADR 0028 R5' semantic dedup.

tier1JaccardCuratorLane.description: PR-4/P0.3 (ADR 0028 R5'/R2' 调和). When true, a Tier-1 cross-slug Jaccard near-dup hit is adjudicated by a curator LLM ({update,merge,create}, no skip; failure → deterministic create) instead of the legacy autonomous dedup gate; also demotes the Tier-2 gate to curator neighbor pre-filter (requires rulesAsReadonlyNeighborsEnabled). Set false only as an explicit rollback to the legacy autonomous gate.

stagingPromotionEnabled.description: ADR 0025 §4.1.5 Stage 5 follow-up: enable multi-view gated promotion of staging promote_candidate entries to durable memory. Default false (opt-in). Requires autoLlmWriteEnabled=true and configured multiView.reviewerProviders to actually run; when false, promote_candidate flags remain advisory and accumulate in staging.

stagingPromotionModel.description: Optional provider/model ref for staging promotion identity resolution (slug/title/statement). Empty means runtime falls back to sediment.classifierModel, then sediment.curatorModel. Concrete model IDs belong in pi-astack-settings.json.

promptVersion.description: ADR 0025 prompt/protocol version tags written into audit rows so classifier and multi-view quality can be grouped by prompt version.

aggregator.description: Aggregator prompt/protocol version. v1.3 (Outcome→Entry feedback M3, 2026-06-04): optional lifecycle_proposal on PROMOTED entry advisories (affirmative channel only — §4.2 independent evidence required) distilled to a read-only entry-lifecycle-proposals.jsonl sidecar; observation-only (prompt §8), never a durable write, deferred gated executor is sole consumer. v1.2 (identity convergence, 2026-06-03) adds Step-7 stable-identity discipline (recurring structural signals carry a stable canonical slug reused across runs / from evolution_hypotheses) plus the evolution-ledger sidecar adoptUnsluggedAlias re-key, so one belief stays one hypothesis row instead of forking message-hash identities. v1.1 (ADR 0025 §4.3 Phase C + L1 Evolution Loop v1, 2026-05-31): prompt-native skeptical-historian LLM pass over 9 input feeds (mechanical_suspicion_signals, raw_distribution_summary, outcome_counterfactual_excerpts, structural_context, prior_aggregator_summaries, classifier_health_window with trend, per_turn_cost_rollup, p15_watchdog_signals, evolution_hypotheses). v0.2 deterministic mechanical path retained as automatic fallback when modelRegistry is absent or LLM call fails. See extensions/sediment/prompts/aggregator-skeptical-historian-v1.md.

archiveReactivationReviewer.description: Archive reactivation reviewer prompt/protocol version. v1 (ADR 0025 §4.6, Stage 2 2026-05-28): prompt-native daily-debounced archived-entry reviewer with keep_archived/reactivate/hard_archive_recommended decisions.

multiView.description: ADR 0025 P0.5 multi-view reviewer settings. Reviewer should be a different provider family from the proposer/curator; current code relies on these defaults and does not enforce provider difference yet.

proposerProviders.description: Reserved for future dynamic proposer selection. Empty means use curatorModel as proposer.

reviewerProviders.description: Ordered reviewer model refs used by multi-view Pass 1/Pass 2. Empty means no reviewer is configured; when autoLlmWriteEnabled is true the runtime emits a one-time advisory/audit instead of silently degrading. Put concrete model IDs in pi-astack-settings.json, not source defaults.

fallbackProviders.description: Additional reviewer refs tried after reviewerProviders.

reviewAllMutations.description: When true, every mutating sediment curator op (create/update/merge/supersede/delete/archive) enters multi-view review. op=skip remains unreviewed. Default false preserves legacy heuristic triggering.

synthesisModel.description: Optional provider/model ref for rich CuratorDecision payload synthesis when Pass 2 confirms an unsynthesizable Pass 1 op (update/merge/supersede/delete). Empty means runtime falls back to sediment.curatorModel. Concrete model IDs belong in pi-astack-settings.json.

persistentInputHistory.description: Persist the editor's ↑/↓ prompt history across pi restarts, per-cwd (extensions/persistent-input-history/). Replaces pi-tui's default in-memory-only, 100-cap, no-persistence history. On every session_start, installs a CustomEditor subclass that preloads `<historyDir>/<sha1(cwd)>--<slug>.jsonl` into the buffer (newest at front, capped maxEntries), silently absorbs pi's renderInitialMessages populateHistory pass via an expectedReplay matcher so the current session's messages are NOT re-appended on every restart, and appends every new submitted prompt with chmod 600. Default `enabled: true` because the extension is pure data-persistence with no LLM-loop side effects. Privacy: every prompt (including expanded paste content) lands on disk in plain text; use /history-compact to dedupe or delete the directory to purge. Composition: calls setEditorComponent unconditionally; cannot coexist with other custom-editor extensions (e.g. modal-editor).

enabled.description: Master switch. When false the extension factory returns early before any hooks register, leaving pi's native in-memory-only history behavior.

historyDir.description: Where per-cwd JSONL history files live. Tilde-expanded if it starts with ~. Chosen at user level (NOT <projectRoot>/.pi-astack/...) because history is keyed by cwd, not project; the same project at different sub-cwds should have independent buffers, and starting pi outside any project should still get history.

maxEntries.description: Hard cap on in-memory buffer length (newest at front). Disk file is NOT auto-truncated; use /history-compact for cleanup.

maxEntryBytes.description: Skip persisting any single entry whose UTF-8 byte length exceeds this. Defends against expanded paste blobs blowing up the JSONL. Keeping each line under PIPE_BUF (~4 KB on Linux) also makes concurrent appends from multiple pi instances safe; default 8192 is a soft compromise.

maxPreloadReadBytes.description: When the on-disk history file exceeds this size, only the tail window of this many bytes is read on preload (and the first, likely-truncated line of that window is discarded). Keeps TUI startup snappy on installations that accumulate huge files.

compactionTuner.description: Trigger pi's compaction at an effective percentage of context budget (extensions/compaction-tuner/). Solves the problem that pi's built-in `compaction.reserveTokens` is an absolute number, while user-stack models span 200k–1M+ contextWindow — a single number can't represent safe headroom uniformly. `thresholdPercent` is a user-configured upper bound; `dynamicThreshold` lowers it by runtime contextWindow / effective model budget (defaults: 272k-class → 60%, 400k-class → 65%, 1M-class → 70%, with 64k min headroom). Hooks `agent_end` as the idle fallback and installs guarded internal patches: `AgentSession._buildRuntime` → `agent.prepareNextTurn` so long tool-call chains can compact after toolResults are persisted but before the next provider request, and `InteractiveMode.handleEvent` so turn-boundary compaction_end is treated as `willContinue: true` and restores the Working loader. `agent_end` still calls public `ctx.compact()`; turn-boundary uses pi's internal `_runAutoCompaction("threshold", true)` to avoid aborting the active run while keeping queued-input semantics safe. Optional `summaryModels` lets `session_before_compact` generate summaries with an ordered provider/model fallback list; empty default preserves pi core behavior, which uses the current main-session model. Pi's built-in threshold (default reserveTokens=16384, ~92% on 200k) remains as a last-resort safety net. Audit log: `<projectRoot>/.pi-astack/compaction-tuner/audit.jsonl`.

enabled.description: Master switch. When false the hook is loaded but short-circuits before any work, leaving pi's native compaction in sole control.

thresholdPercent.description: Configured maximum percent of context budget consumed at which to fire compaction. Clamped to [10, 95]. Provider/model entries in modelPolicies take highest priority; otherwise when dynamicThreshold.enabled is true, the effective threshold is min(thresholdPercent, dynamic cap for the effective context budget, min-headroom cap). Re-evaluated each turn; changes apply immediately on the next turn-boundary or agent_end check with no restart needed.

effectiveContextBudget.description: Optional provider/model-specific effective token budget. Takes priority over dynamicThreshold.modelEffectiveContextBudgets and is capped by the runtime contextWindow if that is smaller.

thresholdPercent.description: Optional provider/model-specific trigger percentage. Takes priority over dynamicThreshold window caps for this provider/model.

minHeadroomTokens.description: Optional provider/model-specific absolute headroom floor. Caps thresholdPercent so at least this many tokens remain.

rearmMarginPercent.description: Optional provider/model-specific hysteresis margin. Overrides the global rearmMarginPercent for this provider/model.

modelPolicies.description: Provider/model → explicit compaction policy overrides, keyed as `provider/modelId`. These policies take priority over dynamicThreshold and global thresholdPercent. If this object is present, it replaces the built-in default map; set {} to remove default per-model policies.

dynamicThreshold.description: Optional dynamic threshold layer. Enabled by default: small/economic windows compact earlier so tool-call chains keep enough headroom before the next model request. `modelEffectiveContextBudgets` lets a provider/model use an economic or observed safe budget instead of its nominal contextWindow. Concrete model-specific budgets belong in pi-astack-settings.json.

enabled.description: Enable dynamic threshold caps. False restores legacy fixed thresholdPercent behavior.

smallWindowMaxTokens.description: Effective budgets up to this value use smallWindowThresholdPercent. Covers 200k/272k-class routes.

smallWindowThresholdPercent.description: Dynamic cap for small/economic windows. 60% of 272k leaves ~109k tokens of headroom.

mediumWindowMaxTokens.description: Effective budgets up to this value use mediumWindowThresholdPercent. Covers ~400k Copilot-style routes.

largeWindowMaxTokens.description: Effective budgets up to this value use largeWindowThresholdPercent. Covers 1M-class models.

minHeadroomTokens.description: Absolute headroom floor. Effective threshold is also capped so at least this many tokens remain, unless the class cap is already lower.

modelEffectiveContextBudgets.description: Provider/model → effective token budget overrides, keyed as `provider/modelId`. The override is capped by the runtime contextWindow if that is smaller. Use for provider-specific safe limits or economic sweet spots. If this object is present, it replaces the built-in default map; set {} to keep no model-specific budget overrides.

rearmMarginPercent.description: Hysteresis margin (percentage points). After triggering at the effective threshold, the trigger disarms until usage drops below `effectiveThresholdPercent - rearmMarginPercent`, then re-arms. Clamped to [0, 90]. Prevents repeated triggers while a long agent loop hovers near the boundary or while compaction is still finishing.

notifyOnTrigger.description: Show a one-line `ctx.ui.notify` message when compaction is triggered (and again on completion / error). The audit row is always written regardless of this flag.

customInstructions.description: Optional custom instructions passed through to pi's compaction LLM (`compact({customInstructions})`). Empty string = pass undefined and use pi's default summary prompt.

summaryModels.description: Optional ordered provider/model refs used by compaction-tuner's custom session_before_compact summarizer. Empty default means do not override: pi core summarizes with the current main-session model. Choose models with strong summarization and long-context compression. If all configured models fail, compaction falls back to pi core default summarization. Ignored when remoteOpenAICompaction.enabled is true; remote OpenAI skip/failure returns undefined so pi core default compaction runs.

remoteOpenAICompaction.description: Optional OpenAI Responses remote compaction path. When enabled, session_before_compact first attempts /responses/compact for explicitly allowlisted OpenAI Responses/Codex Responses models, stores the encrypted compaction item in a recoverable summary marker, and replays it by replacing the marker in before_provider_request. Any skip, failure, unsupported model, missing auth, or invalid response returns undefined from the hook so pi core default compaction remains the fallback.

enabled.description: Master switch for the remote OpenAI compact path. Default false; enabling without an allowlist still skips and falls back to pi core.

modelAllowlist.description: Exact provider/model refs allowed to use remote OpenAI compaction. Empty list is inactive even when enabled, so config mistakes fail closed. Choose models that support the required Responses compaction endpoint.

auditPayload.description: Local sidecar audit mode for parsed /responses/compact payloads. `off` preserves default behavior. `shape` writes a redacted payload shape to `.pi-astack/compaction-tuner/remote-openai-compact-payloads.jsonl` including top-level keys, output item types, and encrypted_content lengths. `full` writes the full parsed compact response, including output item encrypted_content, to that sidecar. The main compaction-tuner audit row only stores payload_audit_id/path/hash/bytes/mode references.

summaryModel.description: Deprecated single-model alias for summaryModels. Kept for transition only; prefer summaryModels for ordered fallback.

ruleInjector.description: ADR 0023-R5/0028 read-only abrain rules catalog injection. Scans ~/.abrain/rules/{always,listed} plus active-project rules and appends compact catalog rows to the main-session system prompt; full rule bodies are read on demand from full_rule_path. This is a read path only: no rule write/promote/veto lifecycle UI.

enabled.description: Enable session-start rules catalog scan + before_agent_start system-prompt injection. Sub-pi still disables via PI_ABRAIN_DISABLED=1.

maxCatalogSummaryChars.description: Maximum must_do_summary characters per injected catalog row. Full rule bodies remain on disk and are read via full_rule_path when needed.

maxCatalogTriggerChars.description: Maximum applies_when / trigger_phrases characters per injected catalog row. This is formatting only, not a write or recall gate.

dualReadAudit.description: ADR 0039 P3a default-off session_start dual-read audit. When enabled, legacy rules remain the only injected runtime truth; the injector also reads constraint-shadow latest artifacts and writes delta rows under ~/.abrain/.state/sediment/constraint-shadow/session-start-dualread/.

enabled.description: Explicit default-off runtime guard. false means no shadow artifact read and no dual-read audit write.

maxReadBytes.description: Maximum bytes read per shadow artifact JSON file during session_start audit. Oversized artifacts record shadow_invalid and do not affect legacy injection.

staleAfterMs.description: Shadow decision file age threshold for marking the dual-read audit row stale. Staleness is diagnostic only and never changes injection.

compiledViewInjection.description: ADR 0039 compiled Constraint view injection. When enabled, the rule injector reads constraint-shadow latest compiled-view artifacts and can fall back to legacy rule injection.

enabled.description: Explicit runtime guard. false keeps legacy rule injection as the only injected truth.

fallbackToLegacyOnError.description: When true, compiled-view read errors fall back to legacy rule injection. Set false only after reconcile and coverage checks are stable.

requireFresh.description: When true, stale compiled views are rejected before injection.

staleAfterMs.description: Maximum compiled-view artifact age accepted by runtime injection.

maxReadBytes.description: Maximum bytes read per compiled-view artifact.

minCoverageRatio.description: Minimum event coverage ratio required before compiled-view injection is accepted.

liveCanary.description: Default-off session-scoped live canary for ADR0039 compiled-view injection. When enabled, only exact matches in sessionIds for persisted sessions with a session file opt into true fail-closed compiled injection: the effective runtime compiledViewInjection is enabled and fallbackToLegacyOnError is false for that session only. Ephemeral sessions and nonmatching sessions keep global behavior.

enabled.description: Explicit default-off guard. false means no session canary override and no session-live-canary audit writes.

sessionIds.description: Exact persisted pi session ids allowed to opt into the live canary. A session id is ignored unless sessionManager.getSessionFile() returns a path, so ephemeral sessions cannot opt in.

propositionPolicyStableViewInjection.description: ADR0040 restricted single-consumer MVP stable-view reader. Success replaces the existing rule injection with one ADR0040 fence. Disabled, unselected, expired, hash-mismatched, or invalid reads return to the unchanged ADR0039 compiled/legacy decision path. The short selection-age window is a controlled MVP canary bound, not complete L1 lifecycle freshness.

enabled.description: One-command rollback guard. false makes this reader unreachable and preserves the existing rule injection path.

selector.description: Exact persisted main-session selector. No wildcard, project, path, or ephemeral-session selector is supported.

session_ids.description: Explicit persisted pi session ids eligible for the ADR0040 MVP reader.

expectedBundleHash.description: Exact content-addressed bundle selected by this runtime authorization. Enabling the reader without this artifact binding is invalid and also fails closed at runtime.

maxSelectionAgeMs.description: Maximum age of the latest symlink publication, measured from the newer of its lstat mtime/ctime. This bounds the MVP canary window only; it does not replace L1 lifecycle freshness.

maxReadBytes.description: Maximum total bytes across the exact five stable-view artifacts. Runtime resolution also clamps to this upper bound.

propositionLifecycleFreshnessD3V2Session.description: ADR0040 D3-v2 session_start single-consumer adapter. Legacy R3.9 enabled mode requires activationObjectPath+activationObjectHash. R4 enabled mode instead requires r4Binding and admits injection only after the exact settings+intent+AUTHORIZED activation+commit receipt runtime gate closes; partial or mismatched selected state is zero-injection with no ADR0039 fallback.

enabled.description: One-command rollback guard. false keeps D3-v2 session_start unreachable. enabled=true requires activationObjectPath+activationObjectHash.

selector.description: Exact persisted main-session selector. No wildcard, project, path, or ephemeral-session selector is supported. Each session_id must be a single safe filename component (R3.8).

items.description: Single safe filename component session id (ASCII [A-Za-z0-9._-], length 1..128, not '.'/'..').

session_ids.description: Explicit persisted pi session ids eligible for the D3-v2 session_start adapter. Each item is a single safe path component (maxLength 128); runtime fail-closes any unsafe entry to disabled/empty selector.

expectedSelectionHash.description: Exact published D3 selection identity authorized for this consumer.

expectedHeadHash.description: Exact published D3 committed head identity.

expectedIntentHash.description: Optional exact published D3 intent identity. When present, runtime compares intent_hash.

adapterManifestHash.description: Exact JCS self-hash (manifest_hash) of the D3-v2 session_start adapter static dependency closure. Live hook must rebuild+validate the current manifest and pass this hash to the reader; settings self-compare alone is not sufficient.

activationObjectPath.description: Absolute path to the bound activation object under the controlled activation root. Required when enabled=true.

activationObjectHash.description: Legacy R3.9 activation self-hash pin. Forbidden in R4 receipt-gated mode.

controlRoot.description: Fixed R4 intent/activation/receipt control root. Runtime never creates it.

settingsPath.description: Exact settings file whose raw postimage is receipt-bound.

maxReadBytes.description: Maximum injectable payload bytes after project-scope filtering.

memory.description: Read-only markdown memory Facade (extensions/memory/). Registers memory_search/get/list/activity/decide over project and optional world ~/.abrain/ stores. The extension never writes memory; sediment remains the sole writer.

includeWorld.description: Whether memory_* read tools should also scan ABRAIN_ROOT (default ~/.abrain) when it exists. Missing world store degrades to project-only without error.

defaultLimit.description: Default result count for memory_search and memory_list.

maxLimit.description: Hard cap on per-call result count.

maxEntries.description: Maximum markdown files scanned per store to keep read tools bounded.

projectBoost.description: Internal ranking multiplier for current-project results. Not exposed in memory_search output.

shortTermTtlDays.description: Dry-run migration policy for legacy .pensieve/short-term/* entries: planned lifetime.expires_at = created + shortTermTtlDays. The read-only extension only reports this plan; it does not write files.

decideModel.description: memory_decide synthesis model. Empty = reuse search.stage1Model (backward-compatible). Set to decouple decision-brief quality from the high-frequency stage1 coarse-recall model; memory_decide is low-frequency and quality-sensitive, so choose a stronger synthesis model when available.

search.description: LLM-driven memory_search settings (ADR 0015 two-stage rerank, extended by ADR 0035 stage0 embedding retrieval + ADR 0036 two-stage collapse). Current pipeline: stage0 = embedding dense retrieval plus sparse BM25 RRF candidate pool (stage0Enabled, default on); stage1 = LLM coarse candidate selection (skippable via stage1Skip); stage2 = LLM full-content rerank (final ranker). The stageN model/limit/thinking fields configure the LLM stages; the stage0* fields configure embedding retrieval. Accuracy is the contract: LLM failures hard-error and there is no grep degradation path.

stage1Model.description: Stage 1 candidate-selection model. Reads the enhanced _index.md candidate surface and outputs top-stage1Limit slug candidates. Choose a fast, low-cost model with reliable broad-recall classification and structured output; deep reasoning is not required. Empty default means fail-closed unless configured in pi-astack-settings.json.

stage1Limit.description: Stage 1 output cap. Wider than stage2Limit so stage 2 has slack to recover from stage 1 misses.

stage1Thinking.description: Reasoning level for Stage 1 candidate selection. Default off because Stage 1 is broad recall/classification. Unsupported levels hard-error rather than silently clamping.

stage2Model.description: Stage 2 final-rerank model. Reads stage 1 candidates' full content and produces ranked top-stage2Limit. This is a reading comprehension and relevance-judgment task; choose a model with strong bilingual relevance ranking and reliable JSON output. Empty default means fail-closed unless configured in pi-astack-settings.json.

stage2Limit.description: Final result count returned to caller. Overrides memory.defaultLimit when memory_search uses LLM path. Stage 2 always runs when stage 1 returns any candidate; accuracy is prioritized over latency/cost.

stage2Thinking.description: Reasoning level for Stage 2 full-content rerank. Default off because ranking is a relevance judgment task, not a logical reasoning task — reading the candidates and sorting by match quality doesn't benefit from chain-of-thought. Unsupported levels hard-error rather than silently clamping.

stage0Enabled.description: ADR 0035 P5: enable stage0 embedding+sparse candidate retrieval as the default production recall surface. Dense embedding retrieval and sparse BM25 are fused via RRF into a bounded candidate pool, replacing full-corpus full-body fan-out. Set false to emergency-rollback to full-corpus full-body.

stage0PoolLimit.description: Dense topN candidate quota. P5 oracle-tuned to 300 to give semantic recall enough headroom (200 dropped some zh/concept queries' baseline picks below dense top-200).

stage0MaxCandidates.description: Hard cap on the hybrid (dense ∪ sparse) candidate union — the primary cost knob. 400 (dense 300 + sparse 100) stays far below full-corpus while preserving coverage.

stage0InsufficientPoolK.description: Safety-net threshold: when the candidate pool is smaller than K, trigger a bounded recall expansion (honors ADR §4 fallback).

stage0EmbedTimeoutMs.description: Short timeout for query embedding. On failure the dense arm is circuit-broken to sparse-only (BM25) rather than blocking search.

stage0StaleFloorRatio.description: ADR 0035 P6 freshness invariant: floor budget (as a fraction of stage0MaxCandidates) reserved for stale/missing (just-written, not-yet-indexed) entries so they cannot be crowded out of the candidate pool by dense/sparse hits. A lower bound, not a cap.

stage1CompactSurface.description: ADR 0035 P8 (dark-launch): feed stage1 LLM a compact surface (meta+title+trigger+summary, ~150 tok/entry) for coarse filtering instead of full body (compiledTruth+timeline), leaving full body to stage2. Reduces stage1 token cost; off by default.

stage1Skip.description: ADR 0036 runtime kill-switch: skip the stage1 LLM candidate-selection layer entirely — stage0 dense top-K feeds stage2 rerank directly (two-stage collapse). Code DEFAULT stays false; settings flips it true (current production). Safety-net retry forces stage1 back on verdict=none / pool<K. Set false here to instant-rollback if recall regresses.

sparseBM25.description: ADR 0036: use char-ngram BM25 (ASCII identifier + CJK bigram + IDF + high-signal-field x3) for the stage0 sparse arm instead of naive substring matching (which returns zero matches for Chinese queries). Read paths only (search/path-A/decide/correction); curator dedup pins this false. Code DEFAULT false; settings flips it true (current production).

bestEffortOnNone.description: When stage2 returns none even after bounded recall expansion, return low-confidence top candidates from the stage0 ranking instead of an empty result. Keep false for callers that require strict empty-result semantics.

queryRouting.description: ADR 0036 P5 (dark-launch): exact-lookup fast path for toolSearch. When the query exactly equals an entry slug, or matches `ADR NNNN`/`adr-NNNN` with a unique hit, return that entry directly and skip both LLM calls. No match falls through to normal retrieval — never suppresses recall. toolSearch only.

dedupChunk0Aggregation.description: ADR 0036 P4 condition 1 (dedup path): aggregate topN by chunk0 (head) vector instead of multiVector max-sim, so distinct entries sharing tail chunks don't surface as false near-duplicate neighbors. Default false (max-sim); sedimentDedup profile pins true. No-op when multiVector is off.

autoReconcile.description: ADR 0036 §10.6: at search time, run a bounded bidirectional (stale-add ∪ orphan-prune) background incremental reconcile so new-device / git-pull / archive-delete / read-only sessions self-converge the embedding index. Steady-state predicate (stale+orphan < min) makes this a zero-cost no-op when the index is fresh. Explicit kill-switch: set false to disable.

autoReconcileCooldownMs.description: Minimum interval between two automatic reconciles (debounce on top of single-flight).

autoReconcileMinBacklog.description: On a non-empty index, only trigger reconcile when stale+orphan reaches this backlog; smaller changes use the search-time bounded fallback instead of firing a full reconcile.

pathA.description: ADR 0026 §3.1 walk-back (2026-05-28) path A: always-on relevant-memory injection. Every turn runs a query-rewriter LLM + memory_search with LLM-side strong cutoff (Stage 2 'relevance_verdict' field). Injects when verdict='has_relevant', skips silently otherwise. Replaces the original decision-point detection design (regex prefilter / 5% LLM gate) which was retired due to cross-language infeasibility + expression openness. Sub-agents are excluded.

enabled.description: Master switch for path A. When false, no rewriter / search / inject runs in before_agent_start; memory_decide tool (path B) is unaffected.

queryRewriterModel.description: Path-A rewriter model. Classifies whether the user message carries usable intent and condenses recent context into a search query. Choose a low-latency model with good bilingual intent rewriting and structured output. Empty default means fail-closed unless configured in pi-astack-settings.json.

queryRewriterTimeoutMs.description: Rewriter LLM timeout. Sized to absorb transient provider slowness without blocking the turn forever. Rewriter failure does NOT propagate — path A silently skips and the main agent flow proceeds.

historyMaxTurns.description: v2 (2026-05-28, post §3.1 walk-back design refinement): the rewriter sees this many recent user/assistant turns as context. Critical for resolving sole-context messages ("刚才那个", "继续", "好就用 X") and for grounding the search query in the actual project/task framing. Set to 0 to revert to v1 single-message behavior (NOT recommended; user directive explicitly requires context-aware rewriting).

historyMaxCharsPerTurn.description: v2: per-turn cap in chars (middle-truncation) before history is embedded in rewriter prompt. Defends against one giant assistant reply or code paste blowing prompt budget.

searchLimit.description: Top-N for path-A search (smaller than memory_decide's 8 because the injection appears in every turn's system prompt; ADR 0024 §6 #5 bias-accumulation concern).

injectMaxEntries.description: Cap on how many entries actually go into the inject block; defensive against search returning more than searchLimit asks.

entryExcerptChars.description: Per-entry compiled_truth truncation. Longer than 800 starts to dominate system-prompt budget for every turn; shorter than ~400 loses the timeline / nuance that makes memories useful.

embedding.description: ADR 0035 P1: stage0 embedding candidate-retrieval config. Embedding endpoints are configured here directly so non-chat models do not appear in the chat model registry. Empty baseUrl/apiKey/model = fail-closed (corpus embedding build errors out).

provider.description: Legacy models.json provider key fallback. Prefer baseUrl/apiKey so embedding models stay out of the chat model registry.

baseUrl.description: OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint base URL, including any required API prefix. Empty = fail-closed unless legacy provider fallback is set.

apiKey.description: Embedding endpoint API key or pi-style resolver string, such as !command or $ENV. Empty = fail-closed unless legacy provider fallback is set.

model.description: Embedding model id for the dedicated endpoint. Empty = fail-closed.

dim.description: Embedding vector dimensionality. Must match the configured embedding model's output dimension.

batchSize.description: Texts per embed request. Set no higher than the configured embedding provider's batch cap.

tpmLimit.description: Tokens-per-minute budget for embed calls (Volc Ark Coding Plan = 600K tpm).

maxRetries.description: Retry count for transient embed failures.

entryEmbedMaxChars.description: Per sub-vector truncation (single-vector = whole entry; multiVector = per chunk).

multiVector.description: ADR 0036 P4: split entries beyond entryEmbedMaxChars into multiple sub-vectors (max-sim retrieval) to defeat truncation. Dark-launch; off by default in code.

multiVectorMaxChunks.description: Max sub-vectors per entry (cost ceiling) when multiVector is on.

forgetting.description: ADR 0031 forgetting-autonomy subsystem. Two orthogonal runtime switches: enabled gates decay evaluation, forgetting-side writes, and mutation; instrumentation gates read-side usage observation only. Anti-runaway ceilings, including the per-batch cap, are build-time welded structural floors. Archive reactivation is independent and remains governed by sediment.autoLlmWriteEnabled.

enabled.description: Master switch for forgetting evaluation and mutation. false = no decay assessment scheduling, zero forgetting-side writes, and zero mutation. Does not control read-side instrumentation and does not gate archive reactivation.

instrumentation.description: Read-side usage instrumentation (retrieval-hit + citation). Pure observation; off = instrumentation short-circuits and writes nothing.

demoteShadow.description: Deprecated legacy migration key. Runtime reads this only when enabled is absent; enabled = decayShadow || demoteShadow || autoDemote. Use enabled instead.

decayShadow.description: Deprecated legacy migration key. Runtime reads this only when enabled is absent; enabled = decayShadow || demoteShadow || autoDemote. Use enabled instead.

autoDemote.description: Deprecated legacy migration key. Runtime reads this only when enabled is absent; enabled = decayShadow || demoteShadow || autoDemote. Use enabled instead.

goal.description: goal extension (impl-plan 2026-06-10 P1a): session-scoped objective set by the user via /goal, re-injected every turn as a compaction-drift guard. Inert until /goal set (C4' authorization). Auto-continue ships separately (PR-7) behind goal.autoContinue.

enabled.description: Kill switch for the whole extension (commands + injection + reconcile/GC).

gcMaxAgeDays.description: Stale materialized-view GC: .pi-astack/goal/<sessionId>.json files older than this (mtime) are removed at session_start; the current session's file is always kept.

defaultMaxContinuations.description: Default budget.max_continuations stamped at /goal set (overridable per-goal via --max-continuations). Consumed by PR-7 auto-continue; recorded-only in P1a.

defaultMaxWallMinutes.description: Default budget.max_wall_minutes stamped at /goal set (overridable via --max-minutes). Consumed by PR-7 auto-continue; recorded-only in P1a.

autoContinue.description: PR-7 kill switch. When true AND a goal is active, agent_end runs a fast LLM judge (achieved/blocked/continue); continue sends a `[pi-goal-continuation goal_id=...]` user message (budget PRE-decremented; sediment demotes the prefix out of user-expressed provenance). Exhaustion/blocked pause the goal with a notify.

judgeModel.description: provider/model for the auto-continue judge (classification-tier task). Empty default means fail-closed unless configured in pi-astack-settings.json.

pendingContinuationStaleMinutes.description: Pending continuation intents older than this many minutes are treated as stale, allowing the auto-continue gate to recover after an unconsumed intent.

workflow.description: workflow extension (ADR 0032 Part B, experimental channel). PR-9 ships /workflow dry-run (deterministic v1 DSL validation, works regardless of enabled); the PR-10 executor is hard-gated on enabled. Flipping either default = promotion = blocked on ADR 0030 evaluation harness (ADR 0032 §5).

enabled.description: Experimental-channel master switch. Default off; dry-run validation works regardless.

readOnly.description: When true (default), any stage declaring mutating:true (or listing mutating tools) FAILS dry-run — tools are never silently stripped. Mutating stages additionally require per-stage mutating:true in the DSL and PI_MULTI_AGENT_ALLOW_MUTATING=1 at runtime (W9 triple-explicit).

defaultModel.description: Model for stages that omit `model` (P3 cost layering, ADR 0032 §9: cheap tier for retrieval/execution stages; advisory-only — never used for correctness adjudication). provider/model format. Empty default means fail-closed unless configured in pi-astack-settings.json.

defaultThinking.description: Thinking level for stages that omit `thinking`.

toolCircuitBreaker.description: Global repeated tool-call circuit breaker. Applies to the current agent run, including dispatch-spawned sub-agents, through pi's shared tool_call hook. It blocks and aborts the current agent only when identical (toolName, normalized args) fingerprints repeat consecutively past the active consecutive threshold, or when the tail of recent fingerprints repeats the same multi-tool cycle past the active cycle threshold. Total repeats are retained for diagnostics only and do not trigger blocking.

enabled.description: Master switch. Env PI_ASTACK_DISABLE_TOOL_CIRCUIT_BREAKER=1 disables regardless of settings. Cycle detection is enabled by default alongside the consecutive guard.

totalThreshold.description: Deprecated/ignored at runtime. Retained only for config compatibility and diagnostics so older settings continue to parse; it no longer blocks and does not influence trip decisions.

consecutiveThreshold.description: Block once the same fingerprint has been requested more than this many times consecutively. Default 4 means the 5th back-to-back identical call trips.

cycleDetectionEnabled.description: Enable tail-pattern detection for repeated multi-tool cycles such as ABABAB... and ABCABC.... Only the tail matters; scattered repeats do not count.

maxCycleLength.description: Maximum cycle length considered by the tail-pattern detector. Length 1 is excluded because consecutive repeats are already handled by consecutiveThreshold. Runtime clamps this to 32 so env overrides cannot exceed the supported bound.

cycleRepeatThreshold.description: Block once the same cycle appears this many full rounds at the tail. Default 5 means the 5th repeated round of a pattern trips. Runtime clamps this to 20 so env overrides cannot exceed the supported bound.

abortOnTrip.description: After returning the blocking tool error, abort the active agent so it stops rather than continuing to spend provider calls.

maxSessions.description: Maximum agent-run states retained by the process before oldest entries are pruned.

maxFingerprints.description: Maximum unique fingerprints retained per agent run before oldest entries are pruned.

thinkingRepeatBreaker.description: Visible-thinking repeat breaker for assistant message_update events. Only visible `thinking_delta.delta` chunks are inspected; final text, `text_delta`, and encrypted reasoning are ignored. The breaker tracks per-agent-run normalized segment fingerprints and trips on consecutive repeats or tail cycles, then aborts the current agent run.

enabled.description: Master switch. Env PI_ASTACK_DISABLE_THINKING_REPEAT_BREAKER=1 disables regardless of settings.

consecutiveThreshold.description: Block once the same normalized thinking segment has repeated more than this many times consecutively. Default 4 means the 5th identical segment trips.

cycleDetectionEnabled.description: Enable tail-pattern detection for repeated visible-thinking segment cycles such as ABABAB... and ABCABC....

maxCycleLength.description: Maximum segment-cycle length considered by the tail detector. Runtime clamps this to 32.

cycleRepeatThreshold.description: Block once the same visible-thinking cycle appears this many rounds at the tail. Runtime clamps this to 20.

minSegmentChars.description: Minimum buffered visible-thinking characters before a segment candidate is fingerprinted. Short deltas are accumulated first; they are not fingerprinted one by one.

maxBufferChars.description: Maximum buffered visible-thinking characters kept per agent run before chunking continues. Runtime clamps this to 16384.

abortOnTrip.description: Abort the active agent after audit/notify when the breaker trips.

dispatch.description: dispatch extension (ADR 0009 + 0027). `maxProviderConcurrency` caps same-provider `dispatch_parallel` workers, while `hub` configures the ADR 0030 caged-live dynamic hub (`dispatch_hub` tool). Repeated tool-call protection now lives in top-level `toolCircuitBreaker` and applies to the current agent run, including sub-agents.

auditRotation.description: Dispatch audit.jsonl generation rotation. Business append remains fail-open and process-local ordering is retained.

maxProviderConcurrency.description: Maximum number of concurrent dispatch_parallel workers allowed for the same provider. Invalid or missing values fall back to 4.

taskGovernor.description: Deterministic per-worker tool-call audit thresholds for dispatch/workflow workers. This does not apply to the main session and does not call an LLM audit. Checkpoint, auditPause, and freshAuth each emit one non-terminal audit/progress event; cumulative tool-call count never terminates a worker.

enabled.description: Kill switch for deterministic sub-agent tool-count audit stages. Default true.

profiles.description: Optional overrides for per-profile non-terminal tool-call thresholds. Omitted profiles use runtime defaults.

workerRunGovernor.description: Phase-1 per-run governance owner for dispatch/workflow workers. Owns one governance termination promise; parent abort and timeout remain external lifecycle boundaries.

abortOnRepeat.description: Abort only on high-confidence exact-tail visible-text cycles. Entropy, n-gram, and length-only aborts are not used.

providerBudgets.description: Provider retry termination uses both a consecutive limit and a rolling observation window. providerRetryWindowLimit must satisfy 1 <= limit < providerRetryWindowSize; invalid values or combinations fall back safely at runtime without changing unrelated valid settings.

providerRetryLimit.description: Maximum consecutive provider retries; default 7 allows retries 1-7 and terminates the worker on retry 8.

providerRetryWindowSize.description: Capacity of the rolling provider retry/progress observation window. Must be greater than providerRetryWindowLimit.

providerRetryWindowLimit.description: Maximum retries in the rolling observation window; the next retry terminates the worker. Must be less than providerRetryWindowSize.

toolObservers.description: Bounded audit-only tool behavior observers. Schema rejection coverage is post_execution_only because pi session events do not expose every pre-execution schema reject.

hub.description: ADR 0030 L2 dynamic hub. A cross-vendor hub LLM proposes a worker assignment, then dispatch_hub really dispatches it (read-only, single-level). NO cost gate (INV-COST-NOT-A-GATE). Default-off kill-switch.

enabled.description: Master switch. Default off -> dispatch_hub tool is NOT registered. Flip true to dogfood (ADR 0030 §4); flipping default-on is a separate data-gated ratify (ADR 0030 §8).

model.description: Explicit hub planning model (provider/model). Empty -> auto-pick a flagship-tier model. Cross-vendor decorrelation from workers is flagged regardless (ADR 0030 §7).

maxWorkers.description: Worker-count ceiling, clamped to [1,8]. A non-tunable-past-8 infra sanity bound, NOT a cost gate (HARD_MAX_WORKERS in hub.ts).

thinking.description: Thinking level for the hub planning call.

defaultModel.description: OpenAI Responses image-generation model id used when the imagine tool call omits `model`. Empty default means fail-closed unless configured in pi-astack-settings.json.

vision.description: Vision tool model selection (extensions/vision/). Auto-selects the strongest vision-capable model from the registry according to modelPreferences.

modelPreferences.description: Ordered preference list, most-preferred first. Each entry is 'provider/idSubstring' — substring match on model id (case-insensitive, version-suffix-tolerant). Models matching earlier entries win; unmatched models still participate, ordered by cost.input descending as a rough capability proxy.

webSearch.description: Web search + fetch tools (extensions/web-search/). Registers two pi tools — web_search and web_fetch — backed by a pluggable WebSearchProvider abstraction. Built-in provider: Brave. Future providers (Google CSE / Kagi / Bing / Serper / Tavily / Jina Reader / SearXNG) plug in via registry.ts switch-case (V1) and a public registerWebSearchProvider() hook (V2). Per ADR 0027 PR-A: these tools are exposed to sub-agents by default so L2 workers can read external environment.

provider.description: Backend provider name. Built-in: 'brave'. V2 will accept names registered via registerWebSearchProvider(). Default: 'brave'.

apiKey.description: Direct API key value, resolved with pi's config-value semantics: '!command' runs a shell command and uses its stdout (e.g. "!jq -r --arg k brave '.[$k]' $HOME/.pi/secrets.json"); '$VAR' / '${VAR}' interpolate env vars; anything else is a literal. Takes priority over apiKeyEnv, letting the key live in a single secrets file instead of an environment variable. Empty/unset falls back to apiKeyEnv. Default: '' (unset).

apiKeyEnv.description: Env var name to read API key from. Default 'BRAVE_API_KEY' for zero-migration compat with the existing brave-search skill. When switching providers, set to that provider's expected key env name (e.g. 'KAGI_API_KEY'). Used only when apiKey is unset.

defaultCount.description: Default search result count when caller omits 'count'. Clamped to 1..20. Default: 5.

timeout.description: Network timeout in ms for both search and fetch. Minimum 1000. Default: 15000.

allowPrivateNetworks.description: SSRF escape hatch. When true, web_fetch may access RFC1918 / loopback / link-local / cloud-metadata IPs (AWS 169.254.169.254 / GCP metadata / localhost / 10.x / 192.168.x / etc). Default: false. Enable ONLY on dev machines where you knowingly want sub-agents to reach local services (Ollama, dev mock servers); enabling this on shared / CI / production machines exposes a SSRF attack surface.

context7.description: Context7 library-docs tools (extensions/context7/). Registers two pi tools — context7_resolve and context7_docs — over Context7's public REST API (Upstash), injecting up-to-date, version-specific library documentation + code examples so the LLM stops hallucinating stale APIs. pi ships no native MCP (docs/usage.md:303), so this is a thin HTTP wrapper, structurally a sibling of webSearch. Sub-agents can opt in via tools= when the target session actually registers them; they remain outside the default sub-agent tool set.

enabled.description: Master enable flag. When false the extension registers no tools. Explicit runtime kill-switch — present in settings.json by policy rather than relying on the code default. Code default is true so projects consuming pi-astack without a context7 section still get the tools.

baseUrl.description: Context7 REST API base (no trailing slash). Override for self-hosted / enterprise endpoints; mirrors the MCP server's CONTEXT7_API_URL. Default: 'https://context7.com/api'.

apiKey.description: Context7 API key, resolved with pi's config-value semantics: '!command' runs a shell command and uses its stdout (e.g. "!jq -r --arg k context7 '.[$k] // empty' $HOME/.pi/secrets.json"); '$VAR' / '${VAR}' interpolate env vars; anything else is a literal. Takes priority over apiKeyEnv. Context7 keys start with 'ctx7sk'. Empty/unset falls back to apiKeyEnv. Default: '' (unset).

apiKeyEnv.description: Env var name to read the API key from when apiKey is unset. Default: 'CONTEXT7_API_KEY'.

timeout.description: Network timeout in ms for resolve + docs calls. Docs reranking can be slower than search. Minimum 1000. Default: 20000.

dispatchTaskGovernorLimits.description: Non-terminal tool-call audit thresholds for a dispatch task-governor profile. checkpoint, auditPause, and freshAuth are each emitted once per run; counts above the final threshold remain audit-only.
